text: The Song of Deborah and Barak

segments.text: On that day Deborah and Barak son of Abinoam sang this song:

When the princes take the lead in Israel, when the people volunteer,
bless the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Listen, O kings! Give ear, O princes! I will sing to the LORD; I will sing praise to the LORD, the God of Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: O LORD, when You went out from Seir, when You marched from the land of Edom, the earth trembled, the heavens poured out rain, and the clouds poured down water.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The mountains quaked before the LORD, the One of Sinai, before the LORD, the God of Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the days of Shamgar son of Anath, in the days of Jael, the highways were deserted and the travelers took the byways.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Life in the villages ceased; it ended in Israel, until I, Deborah, arose, a mother in Israel.
segments.speaker: Deborah

segments.text: When they chose new gods, then war came to their gates. Not a shield or spear was foundamong forty thousand in Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
My heart is with the princes of Israel, with the volunteers among the people.
Bless the LORD!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
You who ride white donkeys, who sit on saddle blankets,
and you who travel the road, ponder
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
the voices of the singers[^17] at the watering places. There they shall recount the righteous acts of the LORD, the righteous deeds of His villagers[^18] in Israel.
Then the people of the LORDwent down to the gates:
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ‘Awake, awake, O Deborah! Awake, awake, sing a song! Arise, O Barak, and take hold of your captives, O son of Abinoam!’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then the survivors came down to the nobles; the people of the LORD came down to me against the mighty.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Some came from Ephraim, with their roots in Amalek; Benjamin came with your people after you. The commanders came down from Machir, the bearers of the marshal’s staff from Zebulun.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The princes of Issachar were with Deborah, and Issachar was with Barak,
rushing into the valley at his heels. In the clans of Reubenthere was great indecision.[^19]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Why did you sit among the sheepfoldsto hear the whistling for the flocks? In the clans of Reubenthere was great indecision.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Gilead remained beyond the Jordan. Dan, why did you linger by the ships? Asher stayed at the coast and remained in his harbors.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Zebulun was a people who risked their lives; Naphtali, too, on the heights of the battlefield.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Kings came and fought; then the kings of Canaan fought at Taanachby the waters of Megiddo, but they took no plunder of silver.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: From the heavens the stars fought; from their courses they fought against Sisera.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The River Kishon swept them away, the ancient river, the River Kishon.
March on, O my soul, in strength!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then the hooves of horses thundered—the mad galloping of his stallions.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ‘Curse Meroz,’ says the angel of the LORD.‘Bitterly curse her inhabitants; for they did not come to help the LORD, to help the LORD against the mighty.’
segments.speaker: The angel of the LORD

segments.text:
Most blessed among women is Jael, the wife of Heber the Kenite,
most blessed of tent-dwelling women.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He asked for water, and she gave him milk. In a magnificent bowl she brought him curds.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: She reached for the tent peg, her right hand for the workman’s hammer. She struck Sisera and crushed his skull; she shattered and pierced his temple.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: At her feet he collapsed, he fell, there he lay still; at her feet he collapsed, he fell; where he collapsed, there he fell dead.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Sisera’s mother looked through the window; she peered through the lattice and lamented:‘Why is his chariot so long in coming? What has delayed the clatter of his chariots?’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Her wisest ladies answer; indeed she keeps telling herself,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
‘Are they not finding and dividing the spoil—a girl or two for each warrior, a plunder of dyed garments for Sisera, the spoil of embroidered garments
for the neck of the looter?’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So may all your enemies perish, O LORD! But may those who love Youshine like the sun at its brightest.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And the land had rest for forty years.
